prompt:
I need the core of our edge reverse proxy rebuilt as something we can actually unit-test. Not interested in wrapping httputil.ReverseProxy and hoping — I want the rewrite rules as pure functions with the HTTP plumbing kept thin on top. Module go-edgeproxy, package proxy, stdlib only. The tests (rewrite_test.go, proxy_test.go) are the spec; they're read-only.

Pure core:
- RewriteRequest(in *http.Request, upstream *url.URL, stripPrefix string) *http.Request — returns a NEW outbound request, never mutates the inbound one (deep-copied headers). Scheme/host/Host header retarget to the upstream. stripPrefix comes off the path only on a segment boundary (/api strips /api/users -> /users and /api -> /, but /apiv2/users stays), then joins onto any base path the upstream URL carries without doubling slashes. Query string untouched. RequestURI cleared so the result is transport-ready.
- Forwarding headers, exactly like our nginx tier does it: X-Forwarded-For appends the client ip (host part of RemoteAddr; unparsable RemoteAddr goes in verbatim) to any existing list with ", "; X-Forwarded-Proto and X-Forwarded-Host are set only when absent (first hop wins) — proto is https iff the inbound request had TLS, host is the inbound Host.
- StripHopByHop(h http.Header) — in-place removal of the RFC hop-by-hop set (Connection, Keep-Alive, Proxy-Connection, Proxy-Authenticate, Proxy-Authorization, TE, Trailer, Transfer-Encoding, Upgrade) PLUS every header named inside the Connection value(s), comma-separated, trimmed, case-insensitive. Applies to the outbound request; end-to-end headers (Authorization etc.) must survive.

Thin server:
- New(upstream *url.URL, stripPrefix string) *Proxy, an http.Handler with an exported Transport http.RoundTripper field (defaults to something sane) so tests inject fakes.
- ServeHTTP: rewrite, round-trip, then relay the upstream response: status + headers (hop-by-hop stripped from the response too, including Connection-listed ones; multi-valued headers copied completely), body streamed and the upstream body always closed.
- Upstream errors map to gateway statuses: context.DeadlineExceeded (wrapped counts) or any net.Error with Timeout() true -> 504; anything else -> 502. text/plain, non-empty body.

go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass. Layout up to you (rewrite.go + proxy.go is how I'd do it).
